title: Fanny Shum
entries: She is an excuse of a teacher, if you can even call her that. She does not teach; instead lecture times are office hours and she expects you to self teach every concept by assigning khan academy and kumon videos

Took algebra with her and this class is a free for all. Attendance not mandatory, you need to worry about quizzes, homework, and the midterm/final. That's it. Office hours run three times a week for when you make up quizzes. EVERYTHING is retakable multiple times.

I love Fanny Shum!! She is so so wonderful and caring! I can feel her passion on math during class and she is so inspirational! Explaining complicated concepts clearly and logically, and she is so patient and welcoming and accessible in office hour! You will definitely not be sleepy during her class!!

Precalc setup was confusing. No actual lectures, just problem sets. Every week at recitations you need to do a ton of problems, and if you get a single one wrong, you fail. No explanation about what you got wrong or why. No calculators allowed. Very stressful. I'm just going to review the material and hope to test into calculus 1.

Very structured lectures which I appreciate. I did not feel like the homework's were adequate preperation for the exams. The exams are very difficult and introduce unfamiliar ways of thinking about concepts under very tight time pressure. Otherwise nice professor, accessible outside of class and clearly very passionate and engaging.

Prof Shum is a great teacher and knows how to explain concepts clearly and in many ways. She clearly likes teaching the subject. Lots of practice through Web Assigns, Written HWs, and weekly quizzes. Test questions were of similar form to those we had seen in class/in practice problems (no surprises!) Hard class, so need to put in the time and work

Fine professor and accessible at office hours if you don't understand concepts. She assigns a lot of work to ensure you achieve mastery of the concepts, so ensure that you are capable of dedicating a sizeable chunk of time to her courses.
Pretty good, though it felt like she slightly rushed the last half of the semester, which is where all the hard topics are. Overall, I would take her class again.

Very structured lectures which was really nice although she does tend to move faster compared to the other professors. Still it is not a problem though and I enjoyed her style of teaching. Textbook says is required but didn't need to read it as the lectures were better for understanding content. Curve at the end based on individual performance.
Good curve. Good lecture. Good office hour. I am bad at math.
Tough exams but good at explaining. Very accessible at Office Hours. She gives good curve based on individual improvement.

Prof. Shum is a great professor. She's always available after class for questions and she explains concepts very clearly. People say that she's a tough grader but I haven't really seen that. Every calc 2 class gets the same amt. of homework, and they're very effective for testing your understanding. Exams are similar to HW. 10/10 recommend!!! :D
The exams were much harder than the homework, quizzes, and examples in class. She does not explain the material thoroughly. The TAs won't help you much as they forgot most of the subject.

She gives lots of work but as long as you stay on top everything will be fine. Complete web assigns directly after the lecture. This is easier because she lets everyone leave early after finishing the lecture. Study before each quiz, and try to maximize that grade. For the final, focus on previous topics just as much as the new topics.

Good professor, but be wary of the heavy workload. She gives 6 assignments a week so you will almost always be busy with this class. Tests are not curved individually, but there is a generous shift of ~5% on final grade if you improve over time and show up (lectures, office hours.) Overall a great professor, but you have to stay on top of work.

You'll have homework 5 days a week in Calc2. Be prepared to devote a lot of time to this class so dont take anything else challenging. Many people fail the finals. knew someone who got arounf 32 on one of them. shes willing to raise ur grade if u improve so if you fail something hope is not lost, but the tests are CHALLENGING.

She gives you everything you need to succeed, there is no reason to compain. She is the most available teacher I've had at nyu, always answering questions and has long office hours that probably boosted my grade a whole letter. Exams are very fair and she explains clearly. You actually have to work in this class though.

she's a great teacher and provides so much organized material to help u succeed in calc 2. requires a lot from u on a weekly basis, but is kinda generous in how many quizzes, hws, etc. she will drop. not an easy teacher, but a very fair, very good one. not super flexible, but understanding. u will always know what to expect

Prof Shum's class is challenging, but worth the effort. Her grading is strict, but the material is engaging. I found unstuckstudy and the free textbook helpful for study prep. Overall, a rewarding experience.

A lot of prelecture work that may get overwhelming. Prelecture videos are around 1 hour long, comparative to the actual lecture. Homework is pretty straightforward, two prelecture quizzes, two webassigns, and one/two post lecture quizzes. Three tests that counts for around 65% of your grade. Her lectures are recorded. Take detailed notes.
